'''Nashal''' was one of three major [[City/Legends|cities]] on [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia's]] [[Double Worlds|Twin World]] of [[Talus/Legends|Talus]].

==Description==
Nashal was an incredibly diverse [[City/Legends|city]] located along the banks of the [[Mephyt River]], in the northeastern reaches of Talus. Originally founded by [[Selonian/Legends|Selonian]] architects, the settlement served in its early days as a neutral ground for meetings between Talus' three native species. As the [[Drall (planet)|Drall]], Selonian, and [[Human/Legends|Humans]] learned to cooperate and communicate more effectively, Nashal became a focal point for cultural exchange. After the construction of Nashal's [[Spaceport/Legends|starport]], the city's reputation as a diplomatic haven grew stronger. The [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] often held peace summits between rival systems in Nashal, and the influx of visitors from around [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] only increased the city's diversity. Nashal's emphasis on high culture was not all-inclusive. The city did have its share of slums, industrial areas, and military zones.<ref name="SWG:N" />

[[File:Nashal.png|thumb|left|200px|Another view of Nashal]]
After the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] abolished the [[Imperial Senate/Legends|Imperial Senate]], Nashal's prominence faded. However, the inhabitants retained a great appreciation for high culture and still believed strongly in peace between all sentient species. In the years to come, Nashal's focus shifted from diplomacy to food and drink. In fact, Nashal was known for producing some of the most successful chefs in the [[Corellian system/Legends|Corellian system]].<ref name="SWG:N" />

Nashal was the starting point of the [[Nashal River Race]].<ref name="SWG:NRT" />

==History==
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Nashal had a small Imperial presence, like other [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] cities.<ref name="SWG:N" /> In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="SWG_timeline">{{SWG_timeline}}</ref> the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] conquered the city. Large numbers of Rebel troops then moved in to hold the city.<ref name="SWG:P28" /><ref name="DC" />

Around the same time, several [[Selonian/Legends|Selonians]] of the [[Neetha Den]] on [[Talus/Legends|Talus]] were captured by [[Talusian Smugglers|pirates]] and kept as hostages in order to exploit the rest of the den.<ref name="SWG:TtT">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Legacy|Travel to Talus|Talus}}</ref> The situation generated [[Anti-Humanism|anti-human]] sentiments among the Selonian population of Talus, and hostile anti-human demonstrations erupted in Nashal. Despite no act of violence against Humans being reported, the local [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|police force]] and [[Cluster World Relations]] temporally issued a travel warning for Nashal.<ref name="C1TCC">{{SonyCite|url=en_US/players/content.vm?id=66901&resource=features|text=Chapter 1: The Corellian Captives}}</ref>

==Residents==
The following individuals were residents or visitors of the city in the time after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]: [[Igbi Freemo]], [[Oecax]], [[Durgur Pyne]], [[Green Laser]], [[Jusani Zhord]], [[Sigrix Slix]], [[Slooni Jong]],<ref name="SWG" /> Officer [[Pex (Imperial PR officer)|Pex]],<ref name="SWG:TTE">''[[Star Wars Galaxies: Rage of the Wookiees]]''</ref> CorSec Agent [[Taarna]],<ref name="SWG:SK">{{GalaxiesNGE}}</ref> and [[Amyva Va'new]].<ref name="SWG:TCOA">{{GalaxiesNGE}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Nashal appeared in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Roleplaying game|massively multiplayer online role-playing game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> In the game, Nashal is one of [[Dearic/Legends|only two]] permanent, non-player built cities on Talus. However, there is a third game-owned settlement which exists in the form of an [[Talus Imperial Outpost|Imperial Outpost]].<ref name="SWG" />
